_comment: California standards catalog. Every entry is a DRAFT: codes and text were compiled from secondary sources because the official CDE PDFs are not reachable from the dev environment. verified stays false until a human checks the entry against the official document (see docs/STANDARDS-VERIFICATION.md). UI must present unverified alignments as drafts.

CTE-HSMT.name: California CTE Model Curriculum Standards — Health Science & Medical Technology sector, Patient Care pathway

CA-HE.name: California Health Education Content Standards (2008) — Injury Prevention & Safety, grades 7-8

standards.text: Health and Safety: demonstrate health and safety practices, policies, and procedures, including the prevention of injury.
Recognize the integrated systems approach to health care delivery services: prevention, diagnosis, pathology, treatment, and rehabilitation.
Understand the range of care from prevention through diagnosis, pathology, and treatment.
Understand the basic structure and function of the human body and relate normal function to common disorders.
Recognize common diseases and disorders of the human body.
Compare normal function of the human body to the diagnosis and treatment of disease and injury.
Evaluate information gathered and connect patient data to the appropriate system of care.
Know the definition, spelling, pronunciation, and use of appropriate terminology in health care.
Analyze the role of self and others in causing or preventing injuries.
Analyze personal behaviors that may lead to injuries or cause harm.
Describe hazards related to sun, water, and ice (environmental conditions).

standards.officialCode: 7-8: Accessing Valid Information (3.x.S — exact sub-number TBD)
standards.text: Identify accurate sources of information about injury prevention and safety.
Use a decision-making process to determine a safe course of action in risky situations.
Develop a personal plan to remain safe and injury-free.
Communications: acquire and accurately use health science and medical technology sector terminology and protocols for communicating clearly.
Career Planning and Management: integrate multiple sources of career information to plan and manage careers in the health science and medical technology sector.
Problem Solving and Critical Thinking: solve predictable and unpredictable work-related problems using critical thinking skills, independently and in teams.
Ethics and Legal Responsibilities: practice professional, ethical, and legal behavior consistent with applicable laws, regulations, and organizational norms.
Leadership and Teamwork: work with peers and professionals in a variety of health care settings, demonstrating leadership and effective teamwork.
